rulings.Case Title: NOVELL FAMILY PARTNERSHIP LP et al VS. ELMIRA NAZHMUDINOVA, TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award [Ccp 1285 Et Seq.] And Notice Of Rescheduling Of Previously Scheduled Hearing (Lr 8.2b(3))
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2013, Line 5, PETITIONERs NOVELL FAMILY PARTNERSHIP LP and ALEX NOVELL's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award [CCP 1285 Et Seq.] And Notice Of Rescheduling Of Previously Scheduled Hearing (LR 8.2b(3)). Hearing required. The Court cannot determine whether all of the documents attached as Exhibit 8-C to the Petition are the arbitration award. There are multiple documents included in Exhibit 8-C, including documents a partial final award, multiple "status reports" and "order approving winding up." No opposition filed. Petitioner should also bring to the hearing a proposed form of order and judgment. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING, INC., A VS. GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Pltf'S Written Discovery, For Appointment Of Discovery Referee, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 11, 2013 Line 1 - CROSS COMPLAINANT BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC A LIMITED and BRANDY HO's Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiff's Written Discovery, For Appointment Of Discovery Referee, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. This matter is Continued to October 1, 2013 at 9 am to be heard by Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH L. SCHATZ VS. ELLIOT B. EVERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 11, 2013 Line 3 - DEFENDANT ELLIOT EVERS' Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Motion is largely moot because the Court has overruled Defendant Evers¿ demurrer (see August 29, 2013 Order Overruling Defendant Evers¿ Demurrer To Complaint, notice of entry into the docket on September 4, 2013). Plaintiff Schatz is entitled to depose Defendant Evers, as he is a central party to the case. Although Defendant would like to defer his deposition until 10 days after he is required to file an Answer to the Complaint, Plaintiff¿s preference to depose defendant sooner is permitted by the Code of Civil Procedure. The fact that issues relating to the attorney-client privilege and/or the length of the deposition may arise during the deposition does not mean that there is no valid purpose for the deposition or otherwise warrant the requested protective order at this time. To the extent that the motion seeks prospective relief for anticipated issues, it is premature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
